methods.description: Process for analyzing sediment samples for total organic carbon (TOC) and elemental composition using ICP-MS.

steps.description: Sediment samples were recovered from the cores and stored in individual zip-lock polyethylene bags.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and store sediment samples in bags

steps.description: The methodology of Bojko and Kabala (2014) was followed for TOC analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform TOC analysis using Bojko and Kabala method

steps.description: Sample digestion was carried out by treating about 0.5 g dried sediment sample with 10 ml 7 M HNO3 at 100 kPa and 121°C for 30 min.
The solution was cooled down and centrifuged at 14,000 RCF for 15 min.
The CRM-601 standard was used as a certified reference sediment to calculate the errors.
steps.searchable_summary: use CRM-601 standard for error calculation

steps.description: The extracts were diluted before analysing the elemental levels in an ICP-MS.
steps.searchable_summary: dilute extracts and analyze with ICP-MS

methods.description: Procedure to estimate potential arsenic volume in clay plug sediments using satellite images and sonar surveys.

steps.description: The bulk volume of clay plugs was calculated by multiplying the surface areas of twenty clay plugs, extracted from Sentinel-2 satellite images, with cross-sectional shape models from four side-scan sonar surveys.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate clay plug volume using satellite images and sonar data

steps.description: Sediment proportion ratios of the three sediment classes (clay, silt, and sand) from core data and complementary cored well data sets were used to assign bulk sediment volumes to the clay plugs.
steps.searchable_summary: assign bulk sediment volumes using sediment proportion ratios

steps.description: The ICP-MS analysis results gave the solid-state arsenic content in each of the three sediment classes, allowing calculation of the potential arsenic volume in each of the clay plugs.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate potential arsenic volume using ICP-MS results
